reasoning: HKU5-CoV-2 was identified in February 2025 as a bat coronavirus capable of binding to human ACE2 receptors. However, as of the knowledge cutoff (May 16, 2025), which is three months post-discovery, no human cases have been reported. Major news outlets, health organizations, and specialized disease monitoring networks (like ProMED) have not flagged any spillover events or mysterious respiratory clusters associated with this virus. Expert commentary indicates it is likely less infectious and pathogenic than SARS-CoV-2. Given the short remaining window of 45 days until the resolution date and the lack of any evidence of transmission to date, the probability of any human cases being reported globally by July 1, 2025, is extremely low.
